blocks.content: Eli had been planning for high school since sixth grade. He made class president by October.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: You've been class president for two years.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: Middle school and high school are two entirely different institutions. This is a new mandate.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: Two people. I won by a significant margin.
Savannah arrived at lunch with Marcus, who was now a junior. He and Kit shook hands the way they always had.

blocks.speaker: Marcus
blocks.content: Gets smaller. Give it a month.
Marcus was right, which Kit noted without saying so.
Their table here hadn't calcified yet. For the first three weeks, where you sat was still negotiable.
